data.title: Dow notched a record close as Wall Street rallied on Powell’s Jackson Hole remarks
data.description:
Wall Street ended higher on Friday with the Dow closing at a record high after investors welcomed Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell’s remarks at Jackson Hole.

The blue-chip Dow (DJI) was the top performer on the day as it finished on top by 1.89%, closing at 45,631. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) rounded out the session at 6,466, as it added 1.52%, and the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was able to advance by 1.88% as it closed at 21,496.

Across sectors, 10 of the 11 S&P groups finished in positive territory, with Consumer Discretionary and Energy leading the advance. Consumer Staples, meanwhile, was the session’s weakest performer. 

Over in the bond market yields shifted lower as investors purchased bonds. The longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) had declined by 7 basis points to 4.25%, and the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) had fallen 10 basis points to 3.69%.

At Jackson Hole, Powell remarked that a shifting balance of risks "may warrant adjusting our policy stance," signaling a potential willingness to lower the central bank’s policy rate if conditions justify it.

"Risks to inflation are tilted to the upside, and risks to employment to the downside — a challenging situation," Powell added.

As a result, the odds of a rate cut in September rose as the chance of a September cut was at ~71% before the speech and rose to ~93% after it.

“Today’s price action is best described by the famed securities analyst Musk, E., who once quoth ‘the most entertaining outcome is the most likely – just not the most entertaining for those most affected’.  All manner of sensible investors were positioned neutrally or bearish into Jackson; cue a new Hole in their accounts as the market ripped it up.  Ether is going To the moon and the not-a-meme-stock-meme-stock Opendoor was one of today’s very best performers.  What stage is this?  Well, we think it’s not the top. We think more to come," Seeking Alpha analyst Alex King of Cestrian Capital stated.

"Powell's dovish Jackson Hole comments suggest the Federal Reserve is ready to cut interest rates in September, which is just what investors were hoping to hear, given the recent slowdown in the labor market. While there is still one more employment report before the September meeting, it's clear the Fed has enough data under its belt to justify a September cut," David Laut, chief investment officer at Abound Financial said.

"Jerome Powell's speech in Jackson Hole paves the way for a September rate cut. Whether it will be 25 basis points or 50 basis points is still unknown, but the idea that the Fed is open to cutting rates is bullish for stocks," Paul Stanley, chief investment officer, Granite Bay Wealth Management stated.

Aside from the headline of Powell's speech at Jackson Hole, the economic calendar was light otherwise.

As for stocks that were on the move, shares of Intuit (INTU) slipped 5% after the financial software firm reported its latest quarterly earnings.

data.title: Lion Group registers over 30M ADSs for potential resale linked to $600M convertible notes
data.description:
• Lion Group Holding (NASDAQ:LGHL) is registering 30.41M American Depositary Shares for resale, representing 76.02B Class A ordinary shares.
  • The ADSs are issuable under senior secured convertible debentures due in 2028 (June and July Notes) held by a selling securityholder.
  • Each ADS represents 2,500 Class A ordinary shares.
  • The debentures are part of a securities purchase agreement signed June 17, 2025, allowing up to $600M in convertible notes issuance.
  • The initial note closing was for $11M, with $10.56M in proceeds.
  • Lion Group will not receive proceeds from the resale of ADSs, only from any warrant exercises.
  • LGHL shares rise 8.9% post-market on Friday.
  • Source: SEC filing

data.title: U.S. drilling rig count ticks lower to resume string of declines, Baker Hughes says
data.description:
The number of total oil and gas drilling rigs in the U.S. fell by 1 to 538, the count's 15th decline in 17 weeks after finishing flat a week ago, Baker Hughes said Friday in its latest weekly survey.

Oil rigs fell by 1 to 411 during the week, while gas rigs were unchanged at 122 and 5 rigs remained classified as miscellaneous.

The total rig count was down 47 rigs, or 8% below this time last year.

Rigs targeting crude oil in the Permian Basin and the Eagle Ford were unchanged at 253 and 28, respectively.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

data.title: TeraWulf raises total $1 billion from convertible notes offering
data.description:
• TeraWulf (NASDAQ:WULF) announced full exercise of the $150M greenshoe option on its 1.00% Convertible Senior Notes due 2031.
  • The additional notes were purchased on August 22, 2025.
  • Total proceeds from the offering now stand at $1B.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Immersion Corporation receives delinquency compliance alert notice from Nasdaq
data.description:
• Immersion Corporation (NASDAQ:IMMR) has received a delinquency compliance alert notice from Nasdaq notifying the company that due to the its failure to timely file its annual report on Form 10-K for the fiscal year ended April 30, 2025, with the Securities and Exchange Commission, the company is not in compliance with Nasdaq’s continued listing requirements.
  • The company determined that it would not be able to file the Form 10-K within the 15-day extension period due to a Barnes & Noble Education audit committee internal investigation that rendered management unable to complete the financial reporting process and preparation of its financial statements for the fiscal year ended April 30, 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: AMD shares snapped six-session losing streak
data.description:
Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD) shares inched up on Friday after six consecutive sessions of fall. The stock was up 2.5% at $167.78.

Chip stocks were in the green on Friday after Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell signaled the Fed will potentially cut rates in September if data warrants.

 

AMD’s shares lost 9.5% in the preceding six sessions. On a year-to-date basis, the semiconductor company advanced nearly 35%, compared to the broader market, which rose by 8.3%.

Looking at Seeking Alpha’s quant rating, AMD has a Hold rating with a score of 3.4 out of 5. The company has been graded A for profitability but is dragged down by a D+ grade for valuation. Wall Street analysts have issued a Buy call for the stock.

However, Seeking Alpha analysts have issued a Buy call for AMD with 24 analysts rating it Buy and above. Eight analysts suggest Hold while three recommend Sell.

"By broadening strategic partnerships with key hyperscalers and holding a nice pricing premium as long as it's possible for the right demand/supply equilibrium, I think that it's just inevitable that AMD will keep eating away at Nvidia's GPU market share over time," pointed out a recent Seeking Alpha analysis.

data.title: Beyond Meat snaps six straight sessions of losses
data.description:
Beyond Meat (NASDAQ:BYND) shares snapped six straight sessions of losses on Friday, as the stock closed 2.5% higher at $2.45.

The company, which produces plant-based meat substitutes, lost about 14% in the preceding six sessions. The stock has lost more than 30% so far this year, compared to the over 8% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index.

BYND is down 40% over the past month. The stock closed 0.8% lower on Thursday at $2.39.

Analysts are mostly bearish on the stock. Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, BYND has a Strong Sell rating with a score of 1.14 out of 5. The company received a B+ for growth, while it got an F in the prospect of profitability and momentum.

Turning to the Wall Street community, no analyst gave BYND a Buy or above. Four analysts have given the stock a Hold recommendation, and five have recommended Sell or lower.

Seeking Alpha analysts also see the stock as a Sell.

Beyond Meat in May posted quarterly results that showed another decline in sales and further deterioration in profitability. The gloomy results also led the company to withdraw its FY25 guidance and warn of the “uncertainty within its operating environment.”

“Management may need to sell some equity here just to offset ongoing cash burn, and that will dilute investors quite a bit at this ever-decreasing market cap. At a time when Beyond Meat needed to start showing some good results to potentially help discussions with lenders, the latest results definitely put the company in an even bigger hole,” noted Seeking Alpha analyst Bill Maurer.

data.title: Apple snaps six straight sessions of losses
data.description:
Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) shares snapped six straight sessions of losses, as the stock was up 1.21% at $227.62 on Friday.

The tech giant lost 3.39% in the preceding six sessions. However, AAPL gained 6.59% over the past one month.

Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) is in early talks to use Google's AI model Gemini to power a revamped version of its voice assistant Siri, Bloomberg News reported, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

Seeking Alpha analyst William Davis rated the stock as Buy, highlighting that, “Strong financials, robust margins, and a healthy upgrade cycle position Apple for sustained growth, with AI features boosting average selling prices.”

Davis added that there are an estimated 270-300 million iPhones that are older than four years. A surge of these customers upgrading their phones to enjoy the latest AI features can drive revenue to Apple through hardware sales, along with subscriptions to its services and partnerships with AI companies.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant rating, AAPL has a Hold rating with a score of 3.47 out of 5. The company received A+ in the prospect of profitability and B+ for revisions, while it got a D- in valuation and a D for growth factor.

The stock has lost nearly 10% so far this year, underperforming the 8.3% rise in the broader S&P 500 Index during the same period.

Turning to the Wall Street community, 16 out of 43 analysts gave AAPL a Buy and above rating. 15 analysts have given the stock a Hold recommendation, while two recommended Sell or lower.

data.title: MasTec, Quanta Services, Bowman Consulting started as Buys at Texas Capital
data.description:
Energy infrastructure stocks MasTec (NYSE:MTZ), Quanta Services (NYSE:PWR) and Bowman Consulting (NASDAQ:BWMN) all were initiated Friday with Buy ratings and respective price targets of $250, $437 and $59 at Texas Capital.

MasTec's (NYSE:MTZ) customers represent the leading providers of power, telecom data, and oil and gas, and the company has benefited from an insatiable demand to build out AI infrastructure and
the associated need for significant power, civil and data infrastructure, and a shift in political interests is re-igniting opportunities across the pipeline market where it stands as a leading beneficiary, according to Texas Capital's Alex Rygiel.

"With its three primary end markets all accelerating in activity, and the anticipated corresponding earnings growth potential, we believe the shares of MTZ have notable upside," the analyst wrote.

Quanta Services (NYSE:PWR) "might be the best, most-attractive security for investors to benefit from the insatiable demand for energy," Rygiel wrote, with the possible re-emergence of oil and gas-related pipeline infrastructure an incremental catalyst to 2026 and 2027 estimates.

Despite a valuation multiple that appears relatively high, the analyst thinks it is justified and that the stock should be considered a core position in most diversified portfolio seeking broad energy exposure.

Bowman Consulting (NASDAQ:BWMN) is a small-cap engineering and consulting firm benefiting from infrastructure-related megatrends coupled with in-organic growth and margin expansion propelling ~20% annual earnings growth over the next several years, Rygiel said.

The analyst believes the company will continue to be a consolidator in the industry and should
maintain financial flexibility through its strong balance sheet and solid cash flow generation.

data.title: NYU’s Aswath Damodaran: Markets will rally until risks show up in earnings and economy
data.description:
Concerns about stock market valuations reaching unsustainable levels have intensified as major indices hit new highs. Aswath Damodaran, professor of finance at the NYU School of Business, suggested that markets may be more trustworthy than experts when it comes to predicting future performance.

“For the last 10 years, we’ve had this conversation over and over again,” Damodaran said in an interview with CNBC. “Markets (SP500), (DJI), (COMP:IND) reach a new high. We say, ‘are markets crazy?’ And experts like me come on, and we say, ‘markets are too rich.’”

Damodaran’s perspective challenges the conventional wisdom that has dominated market analysis in recent years. He noted that over the past three to four years, he has come to believe that “markets maybe are more trustworthy on this than experts are, that their markets seem to figure things out.” This represents a significant shift from his previous stance on market valuations.

According to Damodaran, the market has experienced two distinct phases this year. “Through April 8, tech (XLK) especially was beaten up. The Mag 7 (MAGS) collectively lost, I think, $2T in market cap just in that first three months,” he said, highlighting the substantial volatility that has characterized recent market movements.

When asked about the Federal Reserve’s impact on markets, Damodaran expressed skepticism about the significance of rate cuts. “They don’t even affect the one thing they’re supposed to affect, which is interest rates,” he said. “They might affect the Fed funds rate, but treasury rates (US10Y), (US2Y), (US30Y) themselves have a life of their own.”

He predicted that even with Fed rate cuts, treasury bond rates would likely remain above 4% as long as inflation stays around 2.5-2.7%.

Damodaran also attributed recent market movements to a continuously shifting expectations game that “keeps getting reset for this market, and sometimes just delivering what is expected becomes an unexpected plus,” he said.

This pattern explains why markets often react positively to news that merely meets rather than exceeds expectations, creating what Damodaran describes as “a very strange twist on the expectations game.”
